login.heroTitle: Bring shell branding, themes, and extension points together for each business application.
login.heroDescription: This demo extension shows how a reused host shell can present a different product identity without replacing the entire framework.
login.cardDescription: Start a demo Harbor session for any username without relying on seeded credentials.
login.signInDescription: This is a full page override registered through `builtinPages + systemPages.login`.
login.demoCredentials: Demo mode creates a mock Harbor session for any username through the host bridge.

messages.bridgeNotReady: Host bridge is not ready for extension login.
messages.loginFailed: Unable to sign in to Harbor.

identity.title: Harbor identity
identity.description: This login page is provided by the extension itself, not by host-side loginUi field overrides.

systemPage.title: System page override
systemPage.description: The host still owns the `/auth/login` route, while the extension maps that route to a custom builtin page.

sharedRuntime.title: Shared runtime
sharedRuntime.description: Authentication state, navigation, notifications, and language still come from the same host runtime bridge.

coordination.title: Shell coordination
coordination.description: Branding, menus, and default routes stay aligned through one extension.

themes.title: App-tailored themes
themes.description: Each application can publish its own visual language on top of the same host shell.

bridge.title: Shared runtime bridge
bridge.description: Built-in pages and plugins still reuse the same host runtime modules.

builtinPage.title: Tailwind shared with the host shell
builtinPage.description: This page is compiled inside the extension project, but it still reuses host theme tokens and shared UI styles.

tokens.title: Shared tokens
tokens.description: Uses host theme tokens so colors and surfaces follow the active shell theme.

preset.title: Tailwind preset
preset.description: Compiles utility classes with the shared preset instead of depending on host build output.

registration.title: Builtin registration
registration.description: Registers a React component directly into the host builtin page registry through the extension loader.

items.registration: The extension registers this page as a builtin component using `componentId: extension-harbor-page`.
items.routing: The extension also provides a menu item with `pageType: builtin`, so routing stays inside the host shell.
items.tailwind: Tailwind classes like `bg-background`, `text-foreground`, and `shadow-primary` come from the shared preset.

items.baseStyles: Extension imports @nop-chaos/ui/base.css for shared semantic styles
items.hostRegistration: Host injects extension styles and registers the component

notFound.title: This route is not mapped inside the Harbor workspace.
notFound.description: The host shell routed you to `/404`, but the rendered screen comes from the extension package. This demonstrates a second system page override beyond login.

reasons.loginOverride: Login override proves that an extension can replace entry flows.
reasons.notFoundOverride: 404 override proves that the host can keep route ownership while delegating system views.
reasons.sharedShell: The same shell still handles auth state, menu loading, permissions, and plugin runtime coordination.

home.title: Back to Harbor home
home.description: Return to the extension dashboard page and continue browsing from the default Harbor workspace.

page.title: Open Harbor page
page.description: Jump directly to the extension builtin page registered by the Harbor demo package.

harbor.label: Harbor
harbor.description: Cool coastal colors for shared dashboards and operations screens.
